'''Neil Anthony Harmon''' (born 9 January 1969) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for Great Britain & Ireland as a British Lion on the 1996 Tour to Papua New Guinea, Fiji and New Zealand<ref name="1996 Great Britain Lions tour">[[1996 Great Britain Lions tour]]</ref>{{Circular reference|date=July 2019}} [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], and at club level for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], as {{rlp|pr}}, or {{rlp|sr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
===International honours===
Neil Harmon was a British Lion on the 1996 Great Britain & Ireland Rugby League Tour to Papua New Guinea, Fiji and New Zealand.<ref name="1996 Great Britain Lions tour"/>{{Circular reference|date=July 2019}} He also won five caps (plus one as a substitute) for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in 1997–2001 while at Huddersfield Giants, Bradford Bulls, and Salford City Reds.<ref>{{cite book|first1= Graham|last1= Williams|first2= Peter|last2= Lush|first3= David|last3= Farrar|title= The British Rugby League Records Book|publisher= London League|isbn= 978-1-903659-49-6|year= 2009|pages= 104–105}}</ref>

===Challenge Cup Final appearances===
Neil Harmon played at {{rlp|pr}} in [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s 14-36 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1990 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1989–90 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 28 April 1990, in front of a crowd of 77,729.<ref name="1989-1990 Challenge Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=455|title=1989-1990 Challenge Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2016|accessdate=1 January 2017|archive-date=10 May 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170510181353/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=455|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===County Cup Final appearances===
Neil Harmon appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|HK}} [[Carl Webb (rugby league)|Carl Webb]]) in [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s 16-28 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1987 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1987–88 Rugby Football League season|1987–88 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]] on Sunday 11 October 1987.<ref name="1987-1988 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2647|title=1987–1988 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150417123124/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2647|archive-date=17 April 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Regal Trophy Final appearances===
Neil Harmon played at {{rlp|pr}} (replaced by [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] [[Rowland Phillips (rugby)|Rowland Phillips]] on 79-minutes) in [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]'s 12-2 victory over [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] in the [[1990–91 League Cup (rugby league)|1990–91 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 12 January 1991.
